---
title: "Student Self-Assessment Checklist"
---

Use this page before final submission to check your work against Achieved, Merit, and Excellence expectations.

## How to use

- Tick the statements you can prove with evidence.
- If you cannot prove it, improve your site or documentation.
- Use this together with your development log, testing notes, and evaluation.

## 1) Purpose and audience

### Achieved

- [ ] My website has a clear purpose.
- [ ] I can name my intended audience.
- [ ] My pages match the topic and purpose.

### Merit

- [ ] My content and layout are clearly designed for my audience.
- [ ] My headings, labels, and explanations help users understand quickly.

### Excellence

- [ ] I can justify specific design decisions using audience needs.
- [ ] My site communication is refined and consistently effective.

## 2) Technical quality

### Achieved

- [ ] My navigation works across all pages.
- [ ] All required pages render correctly.
- [ ] My site has no major broken elements.

### Merit

- [ ] My site is consistent across pages (layout, style, readability).
- [ ] My charts/tables are clear and labelled well.

### Excellence

- [ ] My site is polished and robust, with strong usability throughout.

## 3) Environmental data and R integration

### Achieved

- [ ] I loaded an environmental dataset in R.
- [ ] I produced at least one valid chart/table/value in my Quarto site.

### Merit

- [ ] My data output supports the key points I am making.
- [ ] I explain what the chart/table shows in plain language.

### Excellence

- [ ] I use data strategically to communicate clear insights.
- [ ] I justify why my data presentation choices are effective.

## 4) Development process and testing

### Achieved

- [ ] I documented my process over time.
- [ ] I tested my site and made at least one improvement.

### Merit

- [ ] I used peer or user feedback to make multiple improvements.
- [ ] I can show before/after evidence for key changes.

### Excellence

- [ ] My iteration is systematic and evidence-led.
- [ ] I can explain why each major change improved fitness for purpose.

## 5) Evaluation quality

### Achieved

- [ ] I describe strengths and limitations of my final outcome.

### Merit

- [ ] I explain my choices with specific evidence from testing and data work.

### Excellence

- [ ] I critically evaluate limitations and propose realistic, high-value next steps.

## Final pre-submission check

- [ ] Live site link works.
- [ ] Source files are complete.
- [ ] Development log is complete.
- [ ] Testing evidence is included.
- [ ] Evaluation is complete.
- [ ] A/M/E evidence statement is complete.
